```python
def shortest_palindrome(s):
    n = len(s)
    if n == 0:
        return ""
    
    # Find the longest palindromic suffix starting from the end of the string
    # Using the KMP failure function approach
    reversed_s = s[::-1]
    combined = s + '#' + reversed_s
    lps = [0] * len(combined)
    
    for i in range(1, len(combined)):
        j = lps[i - 1]
        while j > 0 and combined[i] != combined[j]:
            j = lps[j - 1]
        if combined[i] == combined[j]:
            j += 1
        lps[i] = j
    
    # The length of the longest palindromic suffix
    longest_palindrome_suffix_length = lps[-1]
    
    # Calculate the characters to add at the beginning
    chars_to_add = s[:n - longest_palindrome_suffix_length][::-1]
    return s + chars_to_add

# Read input
import sys
input_str = sys.stdin.read().strip()
# Compute and print result
print(shortest_palindrome(input_str))
```
